Role: Tech – Software Engineer : Tradenet Rebuild [GDPA]

Essential Requirements

1. Proficient in at least one programming language and comfortable writing clean, maintainable code

2. Basic understanding of web application fundamentals, including frontend–backend interaction, APIs, and HTTP concepts

3. Willingness to work across the stack and learn both backend (Kotlin) and frontend (React) technologies

4. Strong problem-solving skills, curiosity, and the ability to learn independently in a fast-moving engineering environment

Preferred (Good to Have)

1. Prior experience with Kotlin, Java, or JVM-based languages, and/or React or modern JavaScript frameworks

2. Familiarity with modern web development practices such as RESTful APIs, component-based UI design, or design systems

3. Exposure to cloud platforms, CI/CD pipelines, or container-based development will be an advantage

Role: Tech – Data Scientist : Responsible AI
Project Description:
As AI systems are increasingly deployed in real-world products and services, ensuring that they are safe, reliable, and responsible is critical. 
The Responsible AI team, part of GovTech's Artificial Intelligence Tech Practice, focuses on developing technical capabilities to define, measure, and mitigate Responsible AI risks within the Singapore public service. We aim to be at the forefront of the AI safety and responsible AI space through learning, experimentation, and applied research.
We build practical frameworks and tools that translate Responsible AI principles into concrete engineering practices that support real deployment decisions. Some of our past works include RabakBench, KnowOrNot, MinorBench, and LionGuard. More details of our work can be found in our Responsible AI Playbook (https://go.gov.sg/responsibleaiplaybook) and division blog (https://go.gov.sg/aip-medium).
As this is a fast-moving space, you will be assigned to work with one of our team members on an applied research project relating to AI safety, robustness, fairness, interpretability, and/or explainability.
You will be involved in all aspects of this process, from reviewing recent research to developing and deploying practical frameworks and tools, and contributing to knowledge sharing across the public sector, industry, and the open-source ecosystem.
If you’re curious about how Responsible AI, practical engineering, and real-world policy come together and would like to contribute your ideas and skills in this space, you’ll find plenty of opportunities to learn, build, and make an impact with us.
Learning Outcomes from Project:
1. Develop a strong understanding of key Responsible AI dimensions, such as safety, robustness, fairness, interpretability and/or explainability, in the context of public sector AI deployments.
2. Gain hands-on experience designing and building practical frameworks and tools for assessing and mitigating AI risks in real-world AI applications, while gaining technical experience in using and evaluating the latest LLMs.
3. Build end-to-end data science and applied research and engineering skills, including experiment design, data analysis, tool development, and communicating actionable findings.
4. Build applied research capabilities by translating emerging research ideas into practical solutions, and implementing them in a fast-paced, real-world environment.
Prerequisites:
1. Proficiency in Python, with experience in data science/AI engineering projects.
2. Practical experience with LLMs and GenAI (e.g. prompt engineering, fine-tuning, RAG, agentic workflows).
3. Solid understanding of applied NLP and machine learning, including statistical analysis, data visualisation, and feature engineering.
4. Able to work independently, reason through ambiguous problems, and communicate technical ideas clearly.
5. Genuine interest in Responsible AI and a passion for improving public services.
6. (Nice to have)
- Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
- Familiarity with GPU-accelerated frameworks (e.g. PyTorch)
- Experience with containerisation and deployment tools (e.g. Docker, Kubernetes)
- Exposure to MLOps, software engineering practices, or operating ML systems in production
- Prior research experience or academic publications in AI/ML

Role: Business : M.A.P.S: Customer Relationship Management
Project Description:
1. Overview
Project MAPS (Managing All Prospects Seamlessly) is a centralised internal platform designed to streamline the lifecycle of potential engagements, from initial inquiry to formal execution. As an organisation dedicated to engineering digital government, our "sales" pipeline is actually a value-creation pipeline.

This system replaces fragmented tracking methods with a unified view of all incoming opportunities and leads. Its primary goal is to nurture these possibilities until they mature into either a Project Commissioning Memorandum (PCM) for engineering initiatives or confirmed adoption for our Digital Products.

2. Alignment with Vision & Mission
This project is not just an administrative upgrade; it is a strategic enabler of our core mandate.

Empowering our Nation (Vision): By efficiently managing opportunities, MAPS ensures that high-potential initiatives do not fall through the cracks. It allows us to identify and prioritise the technology projects that offer the greatest "possibilities" for our nation.

Making Lives Better (Mission): A streamlined intake process reduces friction. The faster we can move a concept from a "Lead" to a commissioned "Project" or an uptake in a "Digital Product", the faster we can deliver engineering solutions that improve the lives of citizens.
Learning Outcomes from Project:
1. The "Process Detective": Mastering the Art of Digital Workflow Design
The Mission: You will learn to look at a messy, human process (like a conversation about a new project) and translate it into a clean, logical system workflow. You will hunt for bottlenecks and find the "hidden value" that turns a casual lead into a national project.

Formal Learning Outcome:
Competency in Business Process Re-engineering (BPR): The intern will be able to analyse current-state operational workflows, identify inefficiencies, and design future-state processes within the MAPS CRM to optimize the "Lead-to-Value Creation" lifecycle.

2. The "Data Architect": Stewarding the Single Source of Truth
The Mission: Data is the fuel for our engineering engine. Your job is to ensure the engine doesn't get clogged with bad fuel. You will learn how to configure the MAPS system enabling it to serve the organisation.

Formal Learning Outcome:
CRM Proficiency & Data Governance: The intern will increase proficiency in understanding what is necessary in CRM dashboards and executing data hygiene protocols to ensure 100% accuracy in pipeline reporting.

3. The "Value Hunter": Decoding Demand for Digital Government
The Mission: We don't sell; we serve. But we still need to know what our "customers" (government agencies) need. You will learn how to listen to a request.

Formal Learning Outcome:
Stakeholder Engagement & Change Management: The intern will gain experience in facilitating user adoption, gathering user feedback, and communicating technical process changes to non-technical stakeholders. Furthermore, the intern will learn about value chain mapping to determine what is important for MAPS.
Prerequisites:
1. Logical Puzzle Thinking: Ability to organise chaos - We are looking for someone who sees a messy list and instinctively wants to sort, categorise and/or retag it.
2: Process Visualisation: Ability to visualise processes and be able to chart it down into simple documents (e.g.: PowerPoint, Excel, or when able Visio/Miro/Figma).
3: Civic Mindedness: Demonstrate an interest in how technology can improve public services. 
4: Continuous Learning: Willing to pick-up simple digital/tech skills (e.g.: Configuring workflows).

Role: Tech – Product Manager : Mobile Application Platform

Singapore’s workforce relies on over 1.6 million Work Pass Holders to keep the economy running — yet today, many of the essential services meant for them are fragmented across multiple mobile apps, each with different experiences, limitations, and constraints.

This project is part of a major initiative to design and build a single, unified Mobile Application Platform that will eventually replace multiple existing apps with one coherent, scalable, and user-centric platform.
The goal is not just consolidation — it is to fundamentally rethink how migrant workers discover information, complete critical tasks, and stay connected with government services, especially in moments that matter (e.g. compliance actions, welfare support, crisis communication).

As an intern on this project, you will work on a live, high-impact government digital product that is:
- Used by hundreds of thousands of real users
- Designed with strong product thinking, UX principles, and data-driven decision-making
- Built as a platform, enabling multiple teams to launch services faster and more cost-effectively

Learn how to frame problems, define outcomes, and make trade-offs across discovery, delivery, and validation — working on a production mobile platform used by real users, not a hypothetical case.

2. How to define and use the right product metrics
Gain hands-on experience translating product intent into measurable success metrics (e.g. usability, adoption, task completion), and learn how data is used to inform decisions — not just to report numbers.

3. User-centred design for complex and diverse user groups
Learn how to design and evaluate experiences for users with different languages, digital literacy levels, and constraints, and how to validate whether solutions actually work in real-world scenarios.

4. Platform product strategy and value creation
Understand how platform products differ from feature products — including how shared infrastructure, consistency, and scalability create compounding value over time for both users and internal teams.

5. Stakeholder communication and decision-making in a real organisation
Learn how product managers communicate clearly with designers, engineers, and non-technical stakeholders, and how to present insights, risks, and recommendations in a way that drives alignment and decisions.
Prerequisites:
1. Strong interest in product management and user-centred problem solving
Demonstrates curiosity about how digital products are designed, built, and improved, with a genuine interest in understanding user needs and behaviour.

2. Structured thinking and ability to break down ambiguous problems
Comfortable working with open-ended problems, forming clear problem statements, and reasoning through trade-offs even when requirements are not fully defined.

3. Clear written and verbal communication skills
Able to articulate ideas, assumptions, and insights clearly to different audiences, and comfortable documenting thinking and decisions in a structured manner.

4. Comfort with data, logic, and basic analysis

Role: Tech – Product Manager : A11y Playground
Project Description:
Accessibility in web design is essential to ensure equal access to information and services for all, including seniors and people with disabilities. However, learning about accessibility can take up a lot of time, resulting in product teams not understanding the value of accessibility and only seeking minimum compliance. A11y Playground is a public-facing platform that helps public officers explore and learn about accessibility intuitively, motivating them to take action towards improving the accessibility state of their digital services.

As a Product Management Intern, you will play a key role in supporting the scaling of A11y Playground and other Usability & Accessibility (U&A) initiatives. Working closely with the Product Manager (PM) and other cross-functional teams, you’ll be involved in:
•	Validating problem spaces: Supporting user research and synthesising insights to better understand accessibility knowledge gaps, behavioural barriers, and pain points faced by public officers.
•	Exploring new opportunities: Identifying ways to improve self-service learning, increase adoption, and embed accessibility practices into product workflows across agencies.
•	Roadmap planning: Contributing to feature prioritisation, defining success metrics (e.g. adoption, engagement, learning outcomes), and supporting sprint planning and milestone tracking.
•	Prototyping and demos: Collaborating with designers and engineers to prototype interactive learning features (e.g. checklists, simulations, walkthroughs).
Learning Outcomes from Project:
1.	Develop hands-on experience in public sector product management, from discovery to delivery
2.	Learn how accessibility and usability principles are translated into scalable digital solutions
3.	Gain exposure to stakeholder management and cross-functional collaboration in government
4.	Understand how to measure product impact through behavioural change, adoption, and capability-building metrics
5.	Build confidence working in ambiguous problem spaces — navigating evolving requirements, incomplete information, and shifting stakeholder needs
Prerequisites:
1.	Strong interest in product management and user-centred problem solving: Curious about how digital products are designed, built, and improved, with a genuine interest in understanding user needs and behaviour.
2.	Structured thinking and ability to navigate ambiguity: Comfortable breaking down open-ended problems, forming clear problem statements, and reasoning through trade-offs even when requirements are not fully defined.
3.	Clear written and verbal communication skills: Able to articulate ideas, assumptions, and insights clearly to different audiences, and document thinking in a structured manner.
4.	Proactive attitude and ownership mindset: Takes initiative, asks thoughtful questions, and follows through on tasks independently rather than waiting for detailed instructions.
5.	Comfortable with imperfect solutions: Understands that products evolve iteratively, and is willing to test, learn, and refine rather than waiting for perfect conditions before taking action.
